description: The Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) between the EU and Canada faced stiff opposition in Ireland from NGOs and local activist groups concerned about investor-state dispute settlement (ISDS) mechanisms. While Ireland ratified the deal, public sentiment fluctuated significantly as the debate mirrored the growing anti-globalization sentiment of the era.

key_events.description: Wallonia (Belgium) regional parliament temporarily blocks CETA signature, triggering widespread European anxiety.
Irish grassroots movements (e.g., People Before Profit) launch a coordinated campaign against ISDS clauses.
The European Court of Justice issues an opinion clarifying the legality of the agreement's investment court system.
Dáil Éireann ratifies CETA, finalizing the state's official commitment to the agreement.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling scenarios where external international events (like the Wallonia blockade) create volatility in local public opinion against trade agreements.
